title: Hyaluronic acid face serum
subtitle: A lightweight water-based serum with 1% hyaluronic acid and a broad-spectrum preservative
excerpt: Hyaluronic acid serum is one of the simplest water-based cosmetics to make: dissolve 1% hyaluronic acid powder in distilled water, add a preservative, bottle. The result is a clear, slightly viscous serum that feels smooth on skin. Distilled water only; tap water shortens shelf life significantly.
sourceNotes: Hyaluronic acid serum formulation: standard cosmetic industry approach, 1% HA in water. Preservative: Optiphen or equivalent broad-spectrum at 1% (manufacturer-recommended use rate). Distilled or pre-boiled water only to minimise microbial load. Shelf life 3 months with preservative at room temperature.

recipe.yieldDescription: ~100 g serum (fills a 100 ml pump or dropper bottle)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale all ingredients proportionally. Keep the preservative at 1% of the total batch weight.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow 1 hour for the HA powder to fully hydrate before bottling.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 3 months at room temperature with preservative. Discard if the texture changes or the serum becomes cloudy or smells off.

glossaryTerms.definition: A naturally occurring polysaccharide that holds water in the skin. As a cosmetic ingredient it is used at 0.1 to 2% in water-based products to give a smooth, hydrated feel.
An ingredient that draws water to the skin surface from both the atmosphere and deeper skin layers. Hyaluronic acid and glycerin are common humectants in face serums.
A preservative effective against bacteria, yeast, and mould. Required in any water-containing cosmetic product. Without one, a water-based serum will grow microbial contamination within days.

content.text: serum is one of the most straightforward cosmetics to make at home. At 1% in distilled water it gives a clear, lightly viscous serum. Adding glycerin as a secondary
adds slip and improves the feel. A
at 1% keeps the product safe to use for 3 months. Use distilled or freshly boiled and cooled water only; tap water contains minerals and micro-organisms that accelerate spoilage.

items.prepNote: Distilled or freshly boiled and cooled; not tap water
1%; sprinkle onto water surface and leave to hydrate, do not stir immediately

content.text: Weigh the distilled water into the beaker. Sprinkle the hyaluronic acid powder across the surface of the water. Do not stir yet. Leave for 30 minutes; the powder will hydrate and sink. After 30 minutes, stir gently until fully dissolved. The liquid will be clear and slightly more viscous than water.
Add the glycerin and stir well. Add the preservative and stir again for 1 minute. Transfer to a clean, sterilised pump or dropper bottle.
Apply 2 to 3 drops or pumps to clean skin. Pat gently rather than rubbing. Follow with a moisturiser or facial oil to seal in the hydration.
3 months at room temperature. Keep the bottle capped between uses. Discard if the serum becomes cloudy, thickens unexpectedly, or develops an off smell.
